name: ExtensionFormularyItem_Status
title: R5: active | entered-in-error | inactive (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `FormularyItem.status` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`FormularyItem.status` 0..1 `code`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `FormularyItem.status` 0..1 `code`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `FormularyItem.status` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `FormularyItem` is not mapped.
Element `FormularyItem.status` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.comment:
Element `FormularyItem.status` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `FormularyItem` is not mapped.
Element `FormularyItem.status` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This status is intended to identify if the formulary item in a local system is in active use within the formulary.  For example, a formulary might include a medicinal product and the status relates to its inclusion.  At some point in the future, if the status of the formulary item is changed to "inactive" that means that it is no longer valid in the formulary.  But the product itself might still be in active use and might be included in other formularies.
element.isModifierReason: This element changes the interpretation of all descriptive attributes.

element.definition: The validity about the information of the formulary item and not of the underlying product or service itself.
element.comment: This status is intended to identify if the formulary item in a local system is in active use within the formulary. But the product itself might still be in active use and might be included in other formularies.

binding.description: A coded concept defining if the medication is in active use.
